criteria: The agent has read Section 2 completely to understand the formal definitions of selective classification, selector function, coverage, and selective risk.
The agent has read Section 3 to understand the Neyman-Pearson lemma and how it motivates likelihood ratio-based selector scores for selective classification.
The agent has read Section 3.1 to understand how MSP and RLog are interpreted as monotonic transformations of the likelihood ratio p_c(x)/p_w(x).
The agent has read Section 3.2 to understand the proposed Delta-MDS and Delta-KNN scoring methods, including their NP-optimality arguments.
The agent has read Section 3.3 to understand the linear combination strategy (Delta-MDS-RLog and Delta-KNN-RLog) and the role of lambda in combining scores.
The agent has read Section 5 completely to understand the experimental setup, including all datasets, classifiers, baseline methods, and evaluation metrics.
The agent has read Appendix A to understand the precise formula and implementation details of each baseline: MSP, MaxLogit, Energy (T=1), MDS, KNN, RLog, and SIRC.
The agent has read Appendix D to understand the full pseudocode for Algorithm 1 (Delta-MDS) and Algorithm 2 (Delta-KNN) including linear combination variants.

The agent has read Appendix E to understand the complete experimental settings including model variants, feature extraction layer choices, dataset download sources, and ImageNet-C evaluation protocol.
The agent has read Table 6 to check the hyperparameter values of lambda and k for each method and model combination (DFN CLIP, EVA, DistilBERT).
The agent has read Table 1 to check the DFN CLIP results on ImageNet-1K and all covariate-shifted variants (Im-R, Im-A, ObjectNet, Im-V2, Im-S, Im-C).
The agent has read Table 3 to check the EVA supervised learning results on Im-1K, Im-V2, Im-S, and Im-C.
The agent has read Table 2 to check the Amazon Reviews language experiment results for all selector methods under in-distribution and covariate shift conditions.
The agent has read Table 4 to check the ablation experiments on Delta-KNN averaging and linear combination variants using DFN CLIP.
The agent has read Table 5 to check the sample efficiency ablation results varying the fraction of labeled data from 0.1% to 100%.
The plan includes implementing the selective classifier framework with threshold-based selector g_{s,gamma}(x) = 1[s(x) > gamma] (Equations 1 and 2).
The plan includes implementing the coverage and selective risk metrics (Equations 3 and 4) for evaluation.
The plan includes implementing the AURC and NAURC evaluation metrics, including the oracle AURC computation (Equation 7).
The plan includes implementing all seven baseline selector scores: MSP, MaxLogit, Energy, MDS, KNN, RLog, and SIRC.
The plan includes implementing the proposed Delta-MDS method by splitting training features into per-class correct and incorrect sets and computing separate Gaussian statistics.

The plan includes implementing the proposed Delta-KNN method using the average log-distance to the k nearest neighbors in the correct and incorrect feature sets (modified formulation from Section 3.2).
The plan includes implementing the linear combination s(x) = s_distance(x) + lambda * s_logit(x) with hyperparameter lambda as specified in Table 6.
The plan includes loading the DFN CLIP model using the ViT-H/14 variant from the OpenCLIP library for vision experiments.
The plan includes loading the EVA 'Giant' model (patch size 14) from the timm library for supervised vision experiments.
The plan includes fine-tuning DistilBERT on the Amazon Reviews dataset using the official LISA training pipeline with default hyperparameters.
The plan includes loading all seven vision datasets: ImageNet-1K, Im-R, Im-A, ObjectNet, Im-V2 (MatchedFrequency), Im-S, and Im-C (corruption level 5) from their open-source repositories.
The plan includes constructing mixed test sets by combining in-distribution samples (Im-1K) with each covariate-shifted dataset to simulate realistic deployment scenarios.
The plan includes using the CLIP text template 'a real, high-quality, clear and clean photo of a {y}' when computing logit-based selector scores for CLIP.
The code for the threshold-based selector function g_{s,gamma}(x) = 1[s(x) > gamma] has been implemented as formalized in Equation 2.
The code for computing coverage (Equation 3) and selective risk (Equation 4) with 0/1 loss has been implemented.
The AURC metric has been implemented as the area under the risk-coverage curve across all coverage thresholds.
The NAURC metric has been implemented according to Equation 7, including oracle AURC computation g* using ground-truth labels and normalization by the classifier's total error R(f).
The MSP baseline has been implemented as the maximum softmax probability s_MSP(x) = max_k p_theta(y=k|x).
The MaxLogit baseline has been implemented as the maximum raw logit value s_MaxLogit(x) = max_k l^(k).
The Energy baseline has been implemented as s_Energy(x) = log sum_k exp(l^(k)) with temperature T=1.
The MDS (Mahalanobis Distance Score) baseline has been implemented with per-class mean features mu_i and a shared tied covariance matrix Sigma estimated from all training features.
The KNN baseline has been implemented using the negative Euclidean distance to the k-th nearest neighbor in the normalized training feature set.
The RLog baseline has been implemented as the difference between the top two logits: s_RLog(x) = l^(1) - l^(2).
The SIRC baseline has been implemented with MSP as S_1 and L1 norm as S_2, with a = mu_{S2} - 3*sigma_{S2} and b = 1/sigma_{S2}.

The code for splitting training samples into per-class correct feature sets A_c^(i) and per-class incorrect feature sets A_w^(i) based on classifier predictions has been implemented as in Algorithm 1, Step 1.

The code for computing per-class correct means mu_i^c, incorrect means mu_i^w, shared correct covariance Sigma^c, and shared incorrect covariance Sigma^w from the split feature sets has been implemented.
The Delta-MDS score has been implemented as D_MDS(x; mu_i^c, Sigma^c) - D_MDS(x; mu_i^w, Sigma^w) according to Equation 5 and Algorithm 1.
The code for splitting training samples into a global correct feature set A_c and a global incorrect feature set A_w (not per-class) has been implemented for Delta-KNN as in Algorithm 2.

The Delta-KNN average log-distance formulation has been implemented as D_KNN(x; A_c) = -1/k * sum_{i=1}^k log(u_i(phi(x))) and similarly for A_w, using the average over k nearest neighbors rather than the k-th neighbor alone.
The Delta-KNN score has been implemented as D_KNN(x; A_c) - D_KNN(x; A_w) according to Equation 6 and Algorithm 2.
Feature normalization has been applied for all KNN-based methods (KNN and Delta-KNN).
The linear combination selector s(x) = s_Delta-MDS(x) + lambda * s_logit(x) has been implemented for Delta-MDS-RLog as in Algorithm 1 step 17-18.
The linear combination selector s(x) = s_Delta-KNN(x) + lambda * s_logit(x) has been implemented for Delta-KNN-RLog as in Algorithm 2 step 18-19.
The DFN CLIP model (ViT-H/14 variant) has been loaded from the OpenCLIP library with pretrained weights.
The EVA 'Giant' model with patch size 14 has been loaded from the timm library with pretrained weights.
DistilBERT has been fine-tuned on the Amazon Reviews training set using the official LISA repository pipeline with default hyperparameters.
The CLIP text template 'a real, high-quality, clear and clean photo of a {y}' has been used for constructing class concept embeddings T_theta(c) when computing logit-based selector scores.
CLIP logits have been computed as the dot product between the text embedding T_theta(c) for each class concept c and the image embedding phi(x).
Feature extraction for CLIP uses the final layer output of the vision encoder, while feature extraction for EVA and DistilBERT uses the penultimate layer output.
The code for loading ImageNet-1K and all six covariate-shifted variants (Im-R, Im-A, ObjectNet, Im-V2, Im-S, Im-C) from their open-source repositories has been implemented.
The ImageNetV2 MatchedFrequency test set has been used for evaluation.

The ImageNet-C evaluation has been implemented using corruption level 5, averaging results within each of the 4 corruption categories (blur, digital, noise, weather) before averaging across categories.
Mixed test sets combining Im-1K and each covariate-shifted dataset have been constructed for evaluation, following Liang et al. (2024).
The k hyperparameters for KNN-based methods have been set correctly: k=50 for KNN and k=25 for Delta-KNN across all models, as specified in Table 6.

The lambda hyperparameters for linear combination methods have been set per model as specified in Table 6 (e.g., lambda=10 for Delta-KNN-RLog on DFN CLIP, lambda=0.5 for EVA, lambda=0.05 for DistilBERT).
The Delta-MDS-MSP and Delta-KNN-MSP combinations have been implemented for the language experiments on Amazon Reviews (Table 2).
The ablation comparing Delta-KNN with and without averaging (k-th only vs. average of top-k log-distances) has been implemented for DFN CLIP.
The sample efficiency ablation has been implemented by subsampling the training feature set to 0.1%, 1%, 10%, 50%, and 100% of labeled data for Delta-MDS-RLog and Delta-KNN-RLog.
The code for running experiments with ResNet50 (from the official PyTorch repository) trained on ImageNet-1K has been implemented for additional results.
The semantic shift experiments on ImageNet-O, iNaturalist, SUN, and Places datasets with DFN CLIP and EVA have been implemented.
All baseline selector scores and proposed methods have been executed successfully on all vision datasets (Im-1K and covariate-shifted variants) with DFN CLIP without errors.
All selector scores have been executed successfully on Im-1K, Im-V2, Im-S, and Im-C with the EVA model without errors.
All selector scores have been executed successfully on the Amazon Reviews dataset (in-distribution and covariate shift) with the DistilBERT model without errors.
The ablation experiments on Delta-KNN averaging and linear combination variants have been executed on DFN CLIP without errors.
The sample efficiency ablation experiments (varying fraction of labeled data) have been executed for Delta-MDS-RLog and Delta-KNN-RLog on DFN CLIP without errors.
The ResNet50 covariate shift experiments on Im-1K, Im-V2, Im-S, and Im-C have been executed successfully without errors.
The semantic shift experiments on ImageNet-O, iNaturalist, SUN, and Places have been executed successfully without errors.
The execution outcomes match Table 1 (DFN CLIP): Delta-KNN-RLog and Delta-MDS-RLog achieve the best Avg (all) AURC and NAURC, with Delta-KNN-RLog leading overall (AURC 5.01, NAURC 0.163).
The execution outcomes match Table 1: Delta-KNN and Delta-MDS individually achieve roughly 50% lower average AURC/NAURC compared to their standard KNN and MDS counterparts.
The execution outcomes match Table 1: RLog ranks as the third best method on average NAURC, outperforming MSP, MaxLogit, Energy, MDS, KNN, and SIRC as a standalone score.
The execution outcomes match Table 3 (EVA): Delta-MDS-RLog achieves the best average AURC (3.86) and NAURC (0.157) across Im-1K, Im-V2, Im-S, and Im-C.
The execution outcomes match Table 3 (EVA): for supervised models, Delta-MDS-RLog outperforms Delta-KNN-RLog, consistent with the Gaussian Discriminant Analysis argument for MDS-based methods.
The execution outcomes match Table 2 (Amazon Reviews): Delta-MDS-MSP achieves the best in-distribution and covariate shift performance, followed closely by Delta-MDS-RLog and Delta-KNN-MSP.

The execution outcomes match Table 4 (ablation): using the average top-k log-distances in Delta-KNN improves performance over using only the k-th distance (average AURC: 6.03 vs 5.89, NAURC: 0.233 vs 0.225).
The execution outcomes match Table 4 (ablation): Delta-KNN-RLog is the best linear combination for CLIP, outperforming Delta-MDS-Delta-KNN, MSP-RLog, and Delta-KNN-MSP.

The execution outcomes match Table 5 (sample efficiency): Delta-KNN-RLog maintains strong performance with as little as 0.1% of labeled data, while Delta-MDS-RLog degrades significantly at 1% and is not applicable at 0.1%.

The execution outcomes match Table 7 (ResNet50): proposed methods (Delta-MDS-RLog and Delta-KNN-RLog) outperform all baselines on Im-1K, Im-V2, Im-S, and Im-C, consistent with findings on larger models.
The execution outcomes match Table 8 (semantic shift): proposed methods Delta-KNN and Delta-KNN-RLog achieve the best average AURC and NAURC on semantic shift datasets for DFN CLIP.
The execution outcomes match Figure 2: the hyperparameter sensitivity analysis shows that Delta-KNN-RLog results are not highly sensitive to lambda, and k performance plateaus at around k=25.

The execution outcomes match Figure 3: risk-coverage curves show that proposed methods (Delta-MDS-RLog and Delta-KNN-RLog) consistently achieve the most favorable risk-coverage tradeoff across all coverage levels for both CLIP and EVA.
